name: Tesla, Inc.
business: Tesla designs, develops, manufactures, sells, and leases fully electric vehicles and energy generation and storage systems, and increasingly delivers AI-related software and services. It sells directly to customers through its website and company-owned stores, and operates two reportable segments: automotive and energy generation and storage.

exposures.sentence: We generally sell our products directly to customers, and continue to grow our global retail, service and charging footprint to accelerate the widespread adoption of our products.
Powerwall and Megapack are our lithium-ion battery energy storage products.
We sell energy generation systems directly to customers and also through channel partners.
We are also applying our artificial intelligence learnings from self-driving technology to Bots, such as Optimus, a general purpose, autonomous humanoid robot in development.

This includes additional compute hardware to better enable the massive amounts of field data captured by our vehicles to continually train and improve these artificial neural networks for real-world performance.
We currently expect our capital expenditures to be in excess of $20 billion in 2026, driven by our AI initiatives, including investments in compute infrastructure and data centers.

In 2025, we further expanded Cortex, our training cluster at Gigafactory Texas, and announced a new collaboration with Samsung to manufacture advanced semiconductors for AI inference and training in the U.S.
In 2021, we launched our insurance product using real-time driving behavior in select states, which offers rates that are often better than other alternatives and promotes safer driving.

catalysts.sentence: In June 2025, we launched our Robotaxi service, an autonomous ride-hailing platform that harnesses our technology and vehicles.
In 2025, we opened the first Tesla Diner in California, which offers Supercharging as well as Tesla themed food and merchandise.

In 2026, we will be ramping six new production lines across vehicle, Bots, energy storage and battery manufacturing, while further leveraging our existing factory, charging and service center footprints to support future growth.
In 2025, we introduced Megapack 3 and Megablock, our next-generation industrial storage product, and began manufacturing a new residential retrofit solar panel.
Our in-house lithium refinery in Texas, which began operations in January 2026.

catalysts.event: New residential retrofit solar panel customer deliveries began
catalysts.sentence: We began manufacturing a new residential retrofit solar panel in 2025, and began initial customer deliveries in January 2026.
